title: Remove Pneumatic Valve From Tipper
summary: Safely remove the pneumatic valve from the tipper to support valve replacement work. This source-specific runbook covers the documented removal portion only: lockout/tagout, PPE, tool gathering, side panel access if needed, valve cover removal, electrical disconnection, air line disconnection, and valve fastener removal.
when_to_use: Use when the pneumatic valve on the tipper must be removed as part of the documented pneumatic valve replacement/removal procedure in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use for pneumatic valve installation or post-install verification; this source section covers removal only.
Do not use if lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Do not use if connector or air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, gain access to the valve area, disconnect valve electrical and pneumatic connections, and remove the valve fasteners.

safety_notes: Lockout/tagout the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning removal work.
Note where each air line connects before removal.
Stop if the lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2

system_preconditions: Tipper lockout/tagout completed using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2

visual_references.description: Figure 7-9 Pneumatic Valve; use for valve location, valve cover fasteners, and connector identification during removal.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before performing component removal.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 before starting pneumatic valve removal.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out in accordance with the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Energy isolation is completed and the tipper is in a safe maintenance state.
steps.failure_condition: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed or the tipper cannot be placed in a safe maintenance state.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" procedure on page 2.

steps.title: Put on required PPE
steps.purpose: Use the PPE listed by the source before handling access panels, connectors, air lines, and fasteners.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves.
steps.expected_result: Required PPE is in place before removal work continues.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are worn.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE is unavailable or not worn.

actions.label: Don PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on the listed PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if required PPE is not available.

source_evidence.text: Put on the listed PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

Nearby procedure text lists required tools and PPE, including safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm, 3-mm, 5-mm, and 6-mm hex wrenches, a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite, with an estimated time of 45 minutes.

steps.title: Gather required tools and materials
steps.purpose: Prepare the listed tools and materials before opening the tipper and removing the valve.
steps.instruction: Gather the listed tools and replacement-related materials from the source: 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.
steps.expected_result: The required tools and listed material are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: All listed tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more listed tools or materials are unavailable.

actions.label: Collect tools
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed tools and replacement-related materials from the source: 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if required tools or listed materials are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the listed tools and replacement-related materials from the source: 2.5-mm hex wrench, 3-mm hex wrench, 5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite.

Nearby procedure text lists required tools and PPE, including safety glasses, gloves, 2.5-mm, 3-mm, 5-mm, and 6-mm hex wrenches, a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and Loctite, with an estimated time of 45 minutes.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the valve area when the side panel blocks access.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the valve area.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and the valve area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The valve area can be reached without obstruction from the side panel.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed or the valve area remains inaccessible.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the valve area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the pneumatic valve figure for general valve-area location after side panel access is opened.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the valve cannot be accessed after removing the documented side panel fasteners.
Escalate if the side panel cannot be removed using the documented fasteners.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the valve area. [Image support needed: figure or diagram showing the station side panel access area.]

The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, remove four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover, and disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

steps.title: Remove the valve cover
steps.purpose: Expose the pneumatic valve and its connections for removal.
steps.instruction: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place, then remove the valve cover.
steps.expected_result: The valve cover is removed and the valve is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: The valve cover is off and the valve and its connections are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The valve cover cannot be removed or the documented fasteners do not release it.

actions.label: Remove valve cover screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Valve cover area and the four M4 socket-head screws referenced for cover removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the valve cover cannot be removed using the documented fasteners.

source_evidence.text: Remove the four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover in place, then remove the valve cover. [Image support needed: figure showing the valve cover and the four M4 socket-head screws.]

The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, remove four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover, and disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-9 appears on page 144 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_9_1_pneumatic_valve_removal.

steps.title: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector
steps.purpose: Separate the valve electrical connection before removing the valve body.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.
steps.expected_result: The M8 electrical connector is disconnected from the valve.
steps.healthy_condition: The connector is fully disconnected and the valve is no longer electrically connected.
steps.failure_condition: The connector cannot be identified or disconnected without uncertainty or damage risk.

actions.label: Disconnect electrical connector
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: M8 electrical connector location at the edge of the valve.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if connector location cannot be positively identified before disconnection.

source_evidence.text: Disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve. [Image support needed: figure showing the M8 electrical connector location at the edge of the valve.]

The removal steps around this figure instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, remove four M4 socket-head screws holding the valve cover, and disconnect the M8 electrical connector from the edge of the valve.

steps.title: Disconnect the three air lines
steps.purpose: Separate the pneumatic connections while preserving line position information for later replacement work.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the three air lines going to the valve, noting where each line connects before removal.
steps.expected_result: All three air lines are disconnected and their connection positions have been noted.
steps.healthy_condition: Each air line connection point is positively identified before removal and all three lines are disconnected.
steps.failure_condition: Air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection or one or more lines cannot be removed.

actions.label: Identify air line positions
actions.exact_action: Note where each line connects before removal.

actions.label: Disconnect air lines
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the three air lines going to the valve.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Three air line connections and their positions on the valve.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection.
Escalate if the air lines cannot be disconnected using the documented removal approach.

source_evidence.text: Disconnect the three air lines going to the valve, noting where each line connects before removal. [Image support needed: figure showing the three air line connections and their positions on the valve.]
Stop if connector or air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection.

steps.title: Remove the valve mounting screws
steps.purpose: Unfasten the pneumatic valve from the tipper so it is ready for replacement.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M4 socket-head screws holding the valve in place.
steps.expected_result: The pneumatic valve is unfastened from the tipper and ready to be removed for replacement.
steps.healthy_condition: The valve is disconnected and unfastened from the tipper.
steps.failure_condition: The valve cannot be unfastened using the documented screws or remains inaccessible or retained after screw removal.

actions.label: Remove valve mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M4 socket-head screws holding the valve in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Two M4 screws that secure the valve.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the valve cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and connections.

source_evidence.text: Remove the two M4 socket-head screws holding the valve in place. [Image support needed: figure showing the two M4 screws that secure the valve.]
The pneumatic valve is disconnected and unfastened from the tipper, ready for replacement.
Escalate if the valve cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and connections.

success_criteria: The tipper has been locked out and tagged out using the referenced procedure.
The valve area has been accessed as needed.
The valve cover has been removed.
The M8 electrical connector has been disconnected.
The three air lines have been disconnected with their connection positions noted before removal.
The two valve mounting screws have been removed.
The pneumatic valve is disconnected and unfastened from the tipper, ready for replacement.
healthy_conditions: The valve and its connections are visible after cover removal.
Electrical and pneumatic connections are positively identified before disconnection.
The valve is disconnected and unfastened from the tipper.
failure_conditions: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Required PPE or tools are unavailable.
The valve area cannot be accessed using the documented side panel removal.
The valve cover cannot be removed using the documented fasteners.
Connector or air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection.
The valve cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and connections.
escalation_guidance: Use L2_support because the source includes LOTO and component removal work.
Stop if the lockout/tagout cannot be completed using the referenced procedure.
Stop if connector or air line locations cannot be positively identified before disconnection.
Escalate if the valve cannot be accessed or removed using the documented fasteners and connections.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic valve location, valve cover fasteners, M8 electrical connector, and general valve connection layout.
